title: lycopersicon esculentum subsp. typicum
intro: Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. typicum, commonly known as the wild tomato, is a subspecies of the tomato plant that is native to South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. typicum include its ability to support immune function, reduce blood pressure, and act as an anti-cancer agent, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
